data.title: Alipay AI Pay hits 120 million transactions in a week, leading in AI agentic payments in China.
data.description: In Hangzhou, China, Alipay revealed that its AI Pay, a payment method facilitating secure transactions via AI agents, recorded over 120 million transactions in the recent week. This marks a significant achievement for agentic commerce in China, positioning Alipay's AI Pay as the world's leading AI agentic payment service. Introduced in 2025, Alipay AI Pay has diversified its applications, incorporating AI agents in apps and mini programs for physical stores such as Luckin Coffee.
BoE's Pill: There is a need for caution on the last mile.

data.title: NUBURU activates Q1 2026 production ramp for 40 high-power blue laser systems under defense platform strategy.
data.description: In Denver, NUBURU, Inc. (NYSE American: BURU), a company specializing in non-kinetic effects and directed-energy technologies, has begun its Q1 2026 production ramp through subsidiary Lyocon S.r.l. This follows a contract worth around $850,000. The contract involves supplying 40 high-power 450nm blue laser systems for a Dutch agriculture project.

data.title: Quesma tests AI's security capabilities with BinaryAudit, finding only 49% success in detecting threats. Supply-chain attacks are on rise.
data.description: In Warsaw, Poland, Quesma, Inc. has introduced BinaryAudit, an autonomous assessment tool that evaluates AI's capability to identify concealed software threats prior to active harm. The outcomes reveal a mixed bag - showcasing potential strengths and notable constraints. Even with the top-tier model, Claude Opus 4.6, boasting a success rate of just 49%, the AI often misjudged harmless programs as hazardous. This is a critical finding as malicious supply-chain tactics are currently inflicting tangible harm. Significantly, state-backed entities executed a recent attack, seizing control over unspecified systems.

data.title: Bybit offers 14,000 USDT prize pool for Valentine's Day with unique rewards for users, aiming to spread love and celebrate.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE on February 13, 2026, Bybit, ranked as the second-largest cryptocurrency exchange globally based on trading volume, is calling on its community to partake in Valentine's Day celebrations with exciting rewards. Under the theme of "A Better Way to Love," Bybit Fiat and Bybit Pay have collaborated to offer special promotions and activities for the occasion.

data.title: TC Energy files 2025 disclosure, (TSX, NYSE: TRP), in Alberta, Feb. 13, 2026.
data.description: In Calgary, Alberta on February 13, 2026, TC Energy Corporation (TRP on TSX and NYSE) submitted documents to Canadian securities regulators.

data.title: AGMH partners with MusicDog.ai to advance AI ecosystem, focusing on HPC and Web3+AI music, becoming full-stack AI provider.
data.description: AGMH has entered into a Letter of Intent (LOI) with MusicDog.ai to collaborate in the fields of High Performance Computing (HPC), audio Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC), and the Web3+AI music ecosystem. This partnership marks AGMH's transition into becoming a comprehensive AI computing provider, aiming to cover all aspects of the technology stack. This strategic move aligns with the company's goals for growth and innovation in the rapidly evolving tech industry.

data.title: 3E Network names Finland Mikkeli project as Nordic Compute Gateway for AI, focusing on low-carbon energy for TCO.
data.description: The Finland Mikkeli project by 3E Network has been labeled as the Nordic Compute Gateway, focusing on AI-native infrastructure. The project aims to utilize low-carbon energy sources to enhance Total Cost of Ownership (TCO).

data.title: 3 E Network secures $20M equity line agreement for share issuance, to be utilized for AI infrastructure & corporate needs.
data.description: MASK, known as 3 E Network, has recently obtained a US$20 million equity line. This funding will allow the company to issue shares as needed over the course of 24 months. The investment is intended to support the development of AI infrastructure and for general corporate purposes within the organization.

data.title: Arista beats Q4 earnings, raises 2026 sales forecast by 25%, stock rises.
data.description: Arista's stock surged following the release of their Q4 earnings, which exceeded expectations. The prominent cloud networking company also announced a revised sales forecast for 2026, anticipating a growth rate of 25%. This positive news was highlighted in a post titled "Arista Earnings Beat. Cloud Networking Leader Raises 2026 Outlook" on an investment platform.

data.title: Creating a winning culture leads to long-term profits; requires time. Info from Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Establishing a successful culture is essential for leaders, as it brings long-term benefits. However, this process is not immediate and requires time and effort. Ultimately, investing in creating a positive and winning culture within a company leads to increased profits over time. This correlation between a thriving culture and financial returns has been emphasized in various publications, including Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Cango receives $65 million investment from directors, shifts to AI-distributed computing after selling $305 million of bitcoin.
data.description: Cango Inc. announced that two directors will purchase $65 million worth of its stock as part of a major new investment strategy, following the sale of more than half of its bitcoin holdings. The company is diversifying into distributed computing for AI clients to reduce reliance on the volatile bitcoin market. This new investment, along with an additional $10.5 million infusion from its largest stakeholder, will aid in financing this transition. Cango had previously focused on bitcoin mining but has shifted its strategy amid declining cryptocurrency prices. The company raised $305 million by selling 4,451 bitcoins and used the proceeds to repay loans and strengthen its balance sheet. It is now expanding into high-performance distributed computing, with a U.S.-based company being established and staffed in Texas. This move marks a significant shift in Cango's business focus away from its original Chinese car trading operations.

data.title: DraftKings shares plunge on Friday despite record Q4 results. Revenue hits $1.99 billion, up 43%, but conservative outlook for 2026 disappoints investors.
data.description: Despite reporting record fourth-quarter results, DraftKings Inc (NASDAQ: DKNG) saw its shares plummet in pre-market trading on Friday due to a conservative outlook for the upcoming fiscal year. The company met revenue expectations, with fourth-quarter revenue reaching $1.99 billion, a 43% increase year-over-year. Adjusted earnings were reported at 36 cents per share, a significant improvement from the prior-year period's loss of 28 cents per share. However, investors reacted strongly to the fiscal year 2026 guidance, which fell short of estimates. DraftKings projected revenue of $6.5 billion.

data.title: Boston Pizza Royalties Income Fund reported record franchise sales of $976.3 million for 2025. Quarterly franchise sales were $244.4 million. Same Restaurant Sales were up 3.7% for the quarter and 4.7% for the year. Cash flows from operations were $9.9 million for the quarter and $39.7 million for the year. Distributable Cash increased by 5.1% for the quarter and 4.1% for the year. Payout Ratio was 127.3% for the quarter and 101.9% for the year. The Fund's net and comprehensive income was $11.1 million for the quarter and $42.2 million for the year.
data.description: Boston Pizza Royalties Income Fund and Boston Pizza International Inc. reported financial results for the fourth quarter and annual periods in 2025. Franchise sales were $244.4 million for the quarter and $976.3 million for the year, marking increases of 4.3% and 4.8% respectively from the previous year. Same restaurant sales grew by 3.7% for the quarter and 4.7% for the year. Cash flows from operating activities were $9.9 million and $39.7 million for the quarter and year, with distributable cash increasing by 5.1% for the quarter and 4.1% for the year. The fund had a cash balance of $3.9 million at year-end. The Trustees declared a distribution of $0.120 per unit for the period of January 1-31, 2026. The fund has paid out a total of $488.2 million or $29.09 per unit in distributions, including regular monthly distributions and special distributions. The Fund's net and comprehensive income was $42.2 million for the year, with key drivers being franchise sales, royalty income, and distribution income. The Fund had a Payout Ratio of 101.9% for the year, excluding a special cash distribution. Key financial numbers include $976.3 million in franchise sales for 2025, $39.7 million in cash flows from operating activities, and a net income of $42.2 million for the year.

data.title: Valvoline Inc. sponsors Concours in the Hills to benefit Children’s Miracle Network Hospitals in Arizona, raising funds for kids' health.
data.description: A company known for preventive automotive maintenance, Valvoline Inc. (NYSE: VVV), announces their sponsorship of Concours in the Hills, a renowned automotive event in Arizona. Celebrating its twelfth year, the gathering unites car enthusiasts and generates essential funds for children’s health via Children’s Miracle Network Hospitals (CMN), benefiting Phoenix Children’s Hospital. Taking place at Fountain Park in Fountain Hills, Arizona, the event highlights the company's commitment to supporting charitable causes related to automotive passion and children's well-being.

data.title: Monro, Inc. (Nasdaq: MNRO) declares $.28/share cash dividend, payable on March 10, 2026 to shareholders on Feb 24, 2026.
data.description: A provider of automotive repair and tire services, Monro, Inc. (Nasdaq: MNRO), based in Fairport, N.Y., has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $.28 per share on its outstanding common stock, including shares for holders of the Class C Convertible Preferred Stock. The dividend will be paid on March 10, 2026, to shareholders as of February 24, 2026. This announcement was made by the company's Board of Directors.

data.title: Flowserve Corp boosts quarterly cash dividend to $0.22/share, up 5% from previous level. Payable on April 10, 2026.
data.description: Dallas-based Flowserve Corporation, a prominent player in flow control solutions for global infrastructure markets, divulged that its Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share on the Company's common stock. This marks a 5% upsurge from the prior quarterly dividend. The dividend will be disbursed on April 10, 2026, to shareholders recorded as of the market close on March [Specific date not mentioned].
